title: plantago psyllium var. parviflora
intro: Plantago psyllium var. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered plantain, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to Western Europe, Mediterranean region, and North Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and health-promoting properties. The plant's seeds contain mucilage, a gel-like substance that has been used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation, and to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues. Herbal preparations of Plantago psyllium var. parviflora, including capsules, powders, and teas, can be used to support gut health, promote regular bowel movements, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
